---
title: "nitrix.nn.attention"
generated: true
signature-hash: 10dbe6ec5a39b851
description: "Scaled-dot-product / flash attention."
listing:
  id: nitrix-nn-attention-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-nn-attention/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.nn.attention`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Scaled-dot-product / flash attention.

The public entry point [`scaled_dot_product_attention`](nitrix-nn-attention/scaled_dot_product_attention.qmd) supports
three-level backend selection.  Under the two-tier parity contract the
`'jax'` reference is the bit-faithful oracle and `'pallas-cuda'` is the
fused flash path, certified equivalent to the reference ($\text{pallas-cuda}
\approx \text{jax}$) only inside nitrix; downstream consumers pin their own
forward-parity oracle to `backend='jax'`.

Until the fused kernel lands, an explicit or auto-resolved `'pallas-cuda'`
request falls back to the reference with a loud `NitrixBackendFallback`
warning.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `h` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `s` | flattened spatial sample positions |
| `d` | spatial dimension (2 or 3) |
| `t` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `d_v` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-nn-attention-symbols}
:::
